methodsAttempted.query: site:uchile.cl ("inteligencia artificial" OR "ChatGPT" OR "IA generativa") Universidad de Chile estudiantes docencia evaluacion
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl surfaced an official FCFM PDF on generative AI in teaching and learning, several official news/course pages, and general commentary.
Firecrawl surfaced the Faculty of Medicine Humanizar la Inteligencia PDF and a university news page announcing the guide.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uchile.cl "uso de IA" "integridad academica" "Universidad de Chile"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl surfaced official health-education guidance and course/program pages. Course listings were treated as low-specificity evidence and rejected.

artifacts.summary: Discovery found two direct official guidance PDFs: one from FCFM on generative AI in teaching and learning, and one from the Faculty of Medicine on ethical and transformative AI use in health education and research. Broader news and course pages were reviewed as official Universidad de Chile sources but rejected as direct policy evidence because they were news/commentary or course listings rather than current claim-ready policy or guidance.

artifacts.claimText: The FCFM guidance recommends that course teaching teams build a transparency policy with students around possible generative AI uses, including defining when and how the AI tool used should be cited.

artifacts.claimText: The FCFM guidance warns teaching teams that inadequate use of generative AI can create ethics risks such as plagiarism and copyright issues, and security/privacy risks such as insufficient data protection or data use without consent.

citation.publisher: Facultad de Medicina, Universidad de Chile

artifacts.claimText: The Faculty of Medicine guide proposes five principles for AI initiatives in that faculty: transparency and traceability, human supervision and non-delegation of critical judgment, equity and technological justice, academic integrity and responsible authorship, and participatory governance with continuous updating.

artifacts.claimText: For health research publications, the Faculty of Medicine guide suggests declaring the type, timing and purpose of AI use, never attributing authorship to an AI tool, and validating AI-assisted production through human authors.

artifacts.title: Universidad de Chile AI Policy Tracker Report - May 2026
artifacts.summary: This staged run found two official Universidad de Chile unit-level AI guidance documents: FCFM guidance for generative AI in teaching and learning, and Faculty of Medicine guidance for ethical AI use in health education and research. Claims are scoped to the issuing faculties and do not assert a university-wide binding AI policy.
limitations: Claims are limited to FCFM and Faculty of Medicine sources and should not be treated as university-wide binding policy.
The Faculty of Medicine source describes itself as an orienting guide rather than binding regulation.
Official news and course pages were rejected as direct policy evidence where they were not current guidance documents.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
